terms.definition: An optimization method that updates parameters using a gradient estimated from a random subset of training data rather than the full dataset.
SGD with batch size greater than 1 and less than the dataset size; the standard form used in deep learning training loops.
A small random subset of training examples used to compute one approximate gradient and one parameter update.
The number of training examples |B| averaged in one gradient computation; controls noise, memory use, and updates per epoch.
One complete pass through the training dataset, implemented as a sequence of mini-batch updates after shuffling or random sampling.
